headline: Con Edison Announces $2 Billion At-The-Market (ATM) Equity Offering Program
summary: Consolidated Edison, Inc. ("Con Edison") (NYSE: ED) today announced a $2 billion ATM equity offering program pursuant to which it may sell its common shares ($.10 par value). Con Edison has entered into an Equity Distribution Agreement (the "Equity Distribution Agreement") with Barclays Capital Inc., BNY Mellon Capital Markets, LLC, BofA Securities, CIBC Capital Markets, Jefferies LLC, J.P. headline: Vinson & Elkins: Two Good to Be True? The SEC Proposes Optional Semiannual Reporting
summary: By Robert L. Kimball, Katherine Terrell Frank, Alex Bahn, Jon Solorzano, Alexandra M. Lewis, Chloe Schmergel, Josh Rutenberg, Randy Thomas, and Matt Turk On May 5, 2026, the SEC proposed to provide public companies the option of filing interim reports semiannually on new Form 10-S instead of quarterly on Form 10-Q. The proposal would amend Rules 13a-13 […] The post Vinson & Elkins: Two Good to Be True? The SEC Proposes Optional Semiannual Reporting appeared first on CorpGov.

headline: How The Autotrader Group (LSE:AUTO) Story Is Shifting With Lower Targets And Higher Risk
summary: Autotrader Group is back in focus after its fair value estimate was trimmed from £6.73 to £6.54 per share, alongside fresh price target cuts such as JPMorgan moving to 515 GBp from 630 GBp. These shifts align with a broader, more cautious tone in recent research, where analysts are recalibrating what they are willing to pay for the stock while debating how much execution risk to factor in. The following sections outline how to interpret these changing targets and track the evolving story...
